source_jobs.title: Bilingual Family Care Navigator (Spanish)
source_jobs.description:
Bilingual Family Care Navigator (Spanish)

Remote, Anywhere in the US


About AnswersNow:

At AnswersNow, we are trailblazing the future of autism therapy, making it more immediate, accessible, and effective for families everywhere. Our innovative virtual ABA therapy platform is thoughtfully designed by clinicians to recreate the focused, supportive environment of in-person therapy, complete with distraction-free features and interactive activities that enhance engagement and progress.

Our team operates fully remote—meaning you’ll have the flexibility to work from the comfort of home. If you're ready to make a meaningful impact and join a team that's reshaping autism therapy, we’d love to hear from you!



Why this role matters:

AnswersNow is seeking a high-velocity, performance-driven Bilingual Family Care Navigator to join our frontline enrollment and conversion team. While this role sits within Operations, it functions exactly like an inside sales and lead-conversion engine. You will be the very first point of contact for families seeking autism care, responsible for managing a high-volume pipeline, executing rapid outbound call cadences, and converting warm leads into scheduled clinical assessments.

If you thrive in a competitive, KPI-driven environment, love working the phones, and are motivated by driving a pipeline from initial inquiry to a "closed" clinical booking, this role is for you.



Job Details

 - W2 Employee

 - Full-Time

 - 100% Remote


Job Requirements

 - Inside Sales or High-Volume Intake Background: 1–3+ years of experience in an inside sales, high-volume customer intake, enrollment advising, or metric-driven customer success environment where phone time and outreach activity were daily KPIs.

 - Pipeline & Target Orientation: Proven ability to manage a dense CRM sales funnel (HubSpot experience preferred), with a strong focus on tracking conversion rates, call volumes, and pipeline velocity.

 - Consultative Communication & Trust Building: Exceptional verbal stamina and communication skills; ability to build immediate trust over the phone with sensitive families while clearly conveying value.

 - Frictionless Task Execution: Highly organized and details-obsessed; capable of processing technical document workflows (like medical records and insurance forms) rapidly without dropping the ball in a fast-paced environment.

 - Education: Associate’s degree or higher preferred; equivalent professional high-volume outreach or sales experience will be strongly considered.


What You’ll Do

 - Drive High-Velocity Outbound Outreach: Execute high-volume inbound/outbound call, SMS, and email cadences through Aircall to immediately qualify inquiring families, capture database profile details, and aggressively minimize speed-to-lead times.

 - Convert Leads to Scheduled Assessments: Own top-of-funnel conversion metrics by actively nurturing inquiring families through the CRM pipeline, overcoming hesitations, and successfully booking their initial clinical orientations and assessments.

 - Deliver Consultative Benefits Pitching: Review insurance eligibility and clearly articulate complex ABA benefits to families, translating financial and technical jargon into a clear value proposition that drives immediate commitment.

 - Navigate Friction & Secure Prior Authorizations: Move families flawlessly through the intake workflow by uploading medical records, verifying diagnostic criteria, and securing initial insurance assessment authorizations to eliminate process delays.
   


What we Offer

 - $24 - $30 per hour + $1k/month variable!

 - Fully remote – work from anywhere in the U.S.

 - Flexible hours with an async-friendly team culture



More About AnswersNow

AnswersNow welcomes applicants of all backgrounds, experiences, and abilities. We believe a diverse team is a strong team, and are committed to provide a fair and equitable experience for every candidate. If you require reasonable accommodations at any stage,

source_jobs.title: Senior Solutions Architect
source_jobs.description:
Senior Solutions Architect
Remote, Anywhere in the US


About AnswersNow
At AnswersNow, we are trailblazing the future of autism therapy, making it more immediate, accessible, and effective for families everywhere. Our innovative virtual ABA therapy platform is thoughtfully designed by clinicians to recreate the focused, supportive environment of in-person therapy, complete with distraction-free features and interactive activities that enhance engagement and progress.

Our team operates fully remote—meaning you’ll have the flexibility to work from the comfort of home. If you're ready to make a meaningful impact and join a team that's reshaping autism therapy, we’d love to hear from you!


Why this role matters:

We’re hiring a Senior Solutions Architect to design technical systems that are secure, scalable, and trusted by both clinicians and families. This role bridges business needs with resilient technology design.

Architectural decisions define how our platform grows. As a Solutions Architect, you’ll need to see both the big picture and the details, ensuring today’s designs won’t become tomorrow’s obstacles. We prize simplicity in design, systems that can be measured and trusted, and leadership that inspires teams to hold themselves to the highest standards. Your impact will be visible in how well our systems scale and how confident families and clinicians feel in using them.



Job Details

 - W2 Employee

 - Full-Time

 - 100% Remote


Job Requirements

 - 5+ years of solutions architecture experience.

 - Proven record of designing scalable, long-standing systems.

 - Strong knowledge of cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure).

 - Experience with serverless frameworks.

 - Communication skills to make complex ideas clear.

 - A focus on trust, simplicity, and long-term stability in execution.

 - High standards of judgment and accountability — always striving to “deserve” the confidence placed in the platform.


What You’ll Do

 - Define and document system architecture for critical services.

 - Evaluate tools and technologies to meet scaling needs.

 - Create blueprints and diagrams that bring clarity to complex systems.

 - Provide technical leadership across product and engineering.
   
   

What we Offer

 - $130,500- $152,250 annual salary

 - Fully remote – work from anywhere in the U.S.

 - Flexible hours with an async-friendly team culture


More About AnswersNow

AnswersNow welcomes applicants of all backgrounds, experiences, and abilities. We believe a diverse team is a strong team, and are committed to provide a fair and equitable experience for every candidate. If you require reasonable accommodations at any stage, we encourage you to reach out. We’re here to support!

Learn more about us at getanswersnow.com http://getanswersnow.com.

source_jobs.title: VP of Health Plan Partnerships
source_jobs.description:
VP of Health Plan Partnerships
Remote, Anywhere in the US


About AnswersNow
At AnswersNow, we are trailblazing the future of autism therapy, making it more immediate, accessible, and effective for families everywhere. Our innovative virtual ABA therapy platform is thoughtfully designed by clinicians to recreate the focused, supportive environment of in-person therapy, complete with distraction-free features and interactive activities that enhance engagement and progress.

Our team operates fully remote—meaning you’ll have the flexibility to work from the comfort of home. If you're ready to make a meaningful impact and join a team that's reshaping autism therapy, we’d love to hear from you!


Why this role matters:

The Vice President of Health Plan Partnerships is a critical component of AnswersNow’s mission to be the leading solution for high-quality, virtual Autism therapy in the United States, and will be instrumental in executing on the company’s strategy. The role will be responsible for developing, pursuing, and closing new health plan contracts to drive sustainable revenue growth for AnswersNow in line with the company’s national expansion strategy. The VP, Health Plan Partnerships will bring a deep understanding of the health plan market and landscape, a proven track record in closing complex B2B health plan sales, negotiating contracts, and expertise in value-based care (VBC), positioning AnswersNow as the preferred ABA partner for health plans. This role will report to the Senior Vice President, Health Plan Partnerships.


Job Details

 - W2 Employee

 - Full-Time

 - 100% Remote


Job Requirements

 - Bachelor's degree in business administration, healthcare management, or a related experience.

 - 5+ years of proven track record of success in business development, sales, and revenue creation within the healthcare payer business, with emphasis on Medicaid and Commercial lines of business.

 - Strong strategic thinking and analytical skills, with the ability to translate vision into actionable plans and measurable results.

 - Excellent communication, negotiation, and relationship-building skills, with the ability to effectively collaborate with internal and external stakeholders.

 - Passion for healthcare innovation and a commitment to improving access to high-quality, personalized healthcare solutions.

 - Entrepreneurial mindset with the ability to thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic environment and navigate ambiguity with resilience and agility.


NICE TO HAVE:

 - Masters-level degree business administration, healthcare management, or a related field (MBA/MHA preferred).

 - 10+ years of proven track record of success in business development, sales, and revenue creation within the healthcare payer business.

 - Subject matter expertise in Behavioral Health or Applied Behavioral Analysis domains.
   

What You’ll Do

 - Build and manage a robust pipeline of target health plan accounts through direct outreach, conference engagement, referral networks, and strategic relationship cultivation.

 - Close net new health plan deals with a heavy emphasis on value-based arrangements with improved margin.

 - Maximize Medicaid and Commercial Total Addressable Market (TAM) in each state in the company’s national footprint through both Fee-For-Service (FFS) and Value-Based Care (VBC) contracts.

 - Lead health plan contract negotiations, ensuring comprehensive network contracting with Medicaid and commercial plans, competitive reimbursement rates, and favorable terms.

 - Identify market opportunities, trends, and competitive threats to inform business strategies.

 - Thrive as part of a high-performing, collaborative team, fostering a culture of accountability, innovation, and excellence.
   

Success Indicators:

 - You produce meaningful new leads and customer meeting volume consistently each quarter.

 - You sign at least 3-4 net new health plan contracts per quarter.

 - Total addressable market exceeds 50% in all states,

source_jobs.title: Staff Engineer AI/ML
source_jobs.description:
Staff Engineer AI/ML
Remote, Anywhere in the US


About AnswersNow
At AnswersNow, we are trailblazing the future of autism therapy, making it more immediate, accessible, and effective for families everywhere. Our innovative virtual ABA therapy platform is thoughtfully designed by clinicians to recreate the focused, supportive environment of in-person therapy, complete with distraction-free features and interactive activities that enhance engagement and progress.

Our team operates fully remote—meaning you’ll have the flexibility to work from the comfort of home. If you're ready to make a meaningful impact and join a team that's reshaping autism therapy, we’d love to hear from you!


Why this role matters:

We're looking for a Senior Engineering Manager who knows that great engineering leadership is about growing people, removing obstacles, and making the team better — not writing the most code. You've led teams before. You know how to hold a high bar for craft while keeping engineers energized and growing. You understand that a team of four highly effective engineers with strong habits will outperform a team of twelve with unclear expectations.

You'll manage a team of Engineering Team Leads, each of whom runs a squad of full-stack engineers. You own the health of the engineering organization: hiring, performance, culture, delivery, and the processes that keep everything running. You partner closely with the CTO, product leadership, and clinical stakeholders to ensure engineering is building the right things, the right way.

This is a player-coach role in spirit — you should be technically credible and able to engage meaningfully in architecture discussions — but your primary output is team output, not individual code.



Job Details

 - W2 Employee

 - Full-Time

 - 100% Remote


Job Requirements

 - 8+ years of professional software engineering experience, with at least 3 years in a senior or staff-level role

 - Deep expertise in JavaScript and TypeScript in a production full-stack environment

 - Strong command of React, Node.js/Express, and Postgres — you can reason about performance, reliability, and maintainability at each layer

 - Proven experience designing and owning distributed systems or data pipeline architectures on AWS (ECS, RDS, S3, Lambda experience valued)

 - Track record of technical mentorship — you've made engineers around you measurably better

 - Excellent written and verbal communication; you can write an architecture doc, run a design review, and brief a non-technical executive in the same day

 - Experience working in or leading engineering at a growth-stage startup — you understand the balance between velocity and sustainability

 - Familiarity with ETL pipelines, AI/LLM integration patterns, or data-intensive systems is a strong plus

 - Significant experience architecting and deploying production-grade ML/AI systems, including LLM integration and model lifecycle management.

Nice to have:

 - Experience with healthcare or regulated data environments (HIPAA compliance, PHI handling)

 - Familiarity with event-driven architectures, message queues, or streaming pipelines

 - Prior experience as an engineering manager who returned to an IC track (player-coach background welcome)

 - Contributions to open source or engineering blog posts / conference talks


What You’ll Do

Technical Leadership

 - Own and evolve the architecture of our core platform: React frontend, Node.js/Express APIs, Postgres, and AWS ECS infrastructure

 - Drive our JavaScript → TypeScript migration, establishing patterns, tooling, and standards the team can follow

 - Design and extend our ETL pipeline and config-driven AI integration framework, enabling engineers to ship AI-powered features faster and more reliably

 - Lead technical design reviews, create architecture decision records (ADRs), and ensure engineering decisions are documented and defensible

 - Identify and resolve systemic technical debt — not just flagging it,

source_jobs.title: Director of Revenue Cycle Management
source_jobs.description:
Director of Revenue Cycle Management
Remote, Anywhere in the US


About AnswersNow

At AnswersNow, we are trailblazing the future of autism therapy, making it more immediate, accessible, and effective for families everywhere. Our innovative virtual ABA therapy platform is thoughtfully designed by clinicians to recreate the focused, supportive environment of in-person therapy, complete with distraction-free features and interactive activities that enhance engagement and progress.

Our team operates fully remotely—meaning you’ll have the flexibility to work from home, and will never have to report on-site to provide client support. If you're ready to make a meaningful impact and join a team that's reshaping autism therapy, we’d love to hear from you!



About the Role

AnswersNow is seeking a strategic, data-driven Director of Revenue Cycle Management (RCM) to lead and scale our end-to-end revenue cycle operations during a critical stage of company growth. Reporting to executive leadership, this role will be responsible for optimizing financial performance, driving operational excellence, and building scalable processes that support rapid expansion across multiple states and payers.

The Director of RCM will oversee key revenue cycle functions including eligibility and benefits, authorization operations, charge capture, claims management, denial prevention, accounts receivable, payment posting, and revenue integrity initiatives. This leader will work cross-functionally with Clinical Operations, Credentialing, Product, Engineering, Finance, and Data teams to improve reimbursement performance, increase automation, and strengthen reporting capabilities.

The ideal candidate combines deep behavioral health and telehealth revenue cycle expertise with startup agility, strong analytical skills, and a passion for building systems and teams that scale.



Job Details

 - W2 Employee

 - Full-Time (Remote)


Job Requirements

 - 7+ years of progressive healthcare revenue cycle management experience.

 - 3+ years of leadership experience, ideally within behavioral health, ABA, mental health, telehealth, or closely related healthcare settings.

 - Deep understanding of commercial insurance, Medicaid, telehealth reimbursement, and multi-state payer operations.

 - Demonstrated success improving KPIs such as denial rates, net collection rates, clean claim rates, and A/R performance.

 - Experience building or scaling RCM operations in a high-growth or startup environment.

 - Strong analytical skills with experience leveraging data to identify root causes and drive operational improvements.

 - Experience partnering cross-functionally with Product, Engineering, Finance, and Operations teams.

 - Familiarity with healthcare technology platforms, billing systems, EHRs, and reporting tools.

 - Proven ability to lead, develop, and retain high-performing teams.

 - Excellent communication, prioritization, and stakeholder management skills.
   
    

What You’ll Do

 - Own the strategy, performance, and scalability of the end-to-end revenue cycle, including authorizations, claims management, denial prevention, payment posting, and A/R operations.

 - Own key RCM performance metrics including net collection rate, denial rate, clean claim rate, cash collections, and days in A/R.

 - Lead initiatives to improve revenue capture, reduce reimbursement leakage, and optimize financial outcomes.

 - Collaborate with Product and Engineering teams to automate workflows, improve system integrations, and enhance operational efficiency.

 - Collaborate with Clinical Operations and Credentialing teams to ensure payer readiness, enrollment accuracy, and operational alignment.

 - Develop executive dashboards and reporting that provide visibility into financial and operational performance.

 - Contribute to organizational planning by forecasting operational needs and identifying opportunities for scalable growth.

 - Lead root-cause investigations into revenue cyc

source_jobs.title: Delegated Credentialing QA Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
Delegated Credentialing QA Coordinator

Remote, Anywhere in the US


About AnswersNow:

At AnswersNow, we are trailblazing the future of autism therapy, making it more immediate, accessible, and effective for families everywhere. Our innovative virtual ABA therapy platform is thoughtfully designed by clinicians to recreate the focused, supportive environment of in-person therapy, complete with distraction-free features and interactive activities that enhance engagement and progress.

Our team operates fully remote—meaning you’ll have the flexibility to work from the comfort of home. If you're ready to make a meaningful impact and join a team that's reshaping autism therapy, we’d love to hear from you!



Why this role matters:

Ensure integrity, accuracy, and compliance of delegated credentialing processes, specifically for BCBA and related behavioral health providers. This role focuses on quality assurance, audits, and monitoring our providers documents, delegated packets to maintain adherence to regulatory, accreditation, and internal standards, ensuring continuous audit readiness. Work closely with the Senior Manager to establish, prepare and execute training materials. Collaborate with the Enrollment Submission team to manage Medicaid, MCO, and commercial payer applications to ensure stricter adherence to regulatory expectations and optimize application processing efficiency. Partner with leadership and Contracting to facilitate licensure expansion and network growth into new markets.



Job Details

 - W2 Employee

 - Full-Time

 - 100% Remote



Job Requirements

 - Review BCBA and related provider packets for completeness, accuracy, and primary source verification.

 - Conduct routine and ad hoc audits of delegated credentialing files to ensure compliance (organizational, payer, NCQA/CMS).

 - Monitor delegated entities for contractual adherence (turnaround times, documentation, verification).

 - Maintain audit tools, checklists, and reporting dashboards.

 - Collaborate with the Credentialing team for policy alignment .

 - Support pre-delegation and annual delegation audits, including documentation.

 - Ensure policies reflect current regulatory/accreditation standards.

 - Assist with training for internal staff and delegated entities on credentialing requirements.

 - Participate in quality improvement and process optimization efforts

 - Provide comprehensive support for licensure initiatives and facilitate the expansion into new states and payer networks



What you’ll do:

 - Reviewing Provider Credential Packets — Examine BCBA and behavioral health provider files for completeness, accuracy, and proper primary source verification before submission or approval.

 - Maintaining Audit Tools & Dashboards — Build and update checklists, tracking spreadsheets, and reporting dashboards that reflect current audit findings and compliance status.

 - Training Development & Delivery — Work alongside the Senior Manager to create, update, and deliver training materials for internal credentialing staff and delegated entities on compliance expectations.

 - Contribute to Answers Now expansion into new markets by ensuring audit readiness for new state licenses and payer networks.

 - Monitoring Delegated Entities — Track contracted delegated entities to confirm they are meeting turnaround times, documentation standards, and verification obligations outlined in their agreements.

 - Policy Review & Regulatory Alignment — Regularly review internal credentialing policies to ensure they reflect the latest regulatory, accreditation, and payer-specific standards.



What we Offer

 - $72,000- $84,000 annual

 - Fully remote – work from anywhere in the U.S.

 - Flexible hours with an async-friendly team culture



More About AnswersNow

AnswersNow welcomes applicants of all backgrounds, experiences, and abilities. We believe a diverse team is a strong team, and are committed to provide a fair and equitable experience for every candidat

notes: Autism therapy platform with a mix of tech and non-tech roles, fully remote team
